content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — NSCameraUsageDescription is generic and NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is a literal "TODO" placeholder (Info.plist lines 6-9), while no location usage even appears in code (only camera scanning in ScannerView.swift:11).

Pierre: Apple reviewers reject apps with placeholder or non-descriptive purpose strings because they must clearly explain to users why access is needed for a specific feature. Here, "This app needs camera access" doesn't mention document scanning, and "TODO" for location is an obvious leftover that will trigger immediate rejection. Fix by writing specific strings like "SnapScan uses your camera to scan documents" and either implementing a real location feature with a matching description or removing the unused location key entirely.
